headline: What American Express (AXP)'s Bigger Dividend and New World Trade Center HQ Plan Means For Shareholders
summary: In recent days, American Express’s board approved a quarterly dividend increase of US$0.13 to US$0.95 per share, payable on May 8, 2026, while also advancing plans for a new two million square-foot global headquarters at 2 World Trade Center in Lower Manhattan, expected to open in 2031. Together, the higher cash payout and long-term headquarters investment highlight American Express’s emphasis on rewarding shareholders today while committing capital to a modern, tech-enabled workplace that...

headline: American Express Taps AI And Sports Deals As Stock Trades Below Targets
summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) has partnered with AI company Traversal to apply enterprise artificial intelligence to site reliability engineering. The company is also expanding its role in sports and entertainment through new long term payments partnerships with major venues and teams. These moves reflect a broader shift toward technology driven operations and deeper brand exposure in key metropolitan markets. American Express, trading at $311.21, is pairing this tech push with a long track...

headline: American Express Company (AXP) Gained from Demand for Its Platinum Card
summary: Bretton Capital Management, an investment management company, released the “Bretton Fund” fourth-quarter 2025 investor letter. A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. Bretton Fund returned 1.44% in Q4 2025 compared to 2.66% for the S&P 500 Index. In 2025, the Fund returned 11.58% compared to 17.88% for the Index. Although the market fluctuates daily […]
